target.name: Corintis
target.founder: Remco van Erp, Sam Harrison, Elison Matioli

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 22.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Corintis is a Series A semiconductor company founded by Remco van Erp, Sam Harrison, and Elison Matioli, having raised $25M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 8 private frontier-chip companies and 3 public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two methods triangulate fair value. Stage-Progression applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $25M raised proxy, implying $50M. Peer-Median anchors to four same-sector, same-stage companies—Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M)—yielding a median of $1.5B and a range of $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring (P/S multiple method) is not applicable; while public semiconductors trade at a 22.3x median price-to-sales, Corintis lacks disclosed revenue data required for the calculation.

The triangulated valuation range is $42M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $775M. The wide dispersion reflects acute uncertainty in early-stage deep-tech: Stage-Progression yields a conservative floor grounded in capital deployed, while Peer-Median pulls toward the upper range based on recent private-market momentum in AI-focused chip design. Material unknowns include product differentiation, go-to-market timeline, and gross margins relative to the peer set. The median estimate weights both methods equally but is most credible within the $200M–$1.2B band, bracketing Stage-Progression and the peer distribution's middle quartiles.
